sentence_reviews.text: Provenance and citation tracking attach claims to source identifiers, retrieved passages, transformations, timestamps, and evidence paths.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA and RAG sources support evidence retrieval and grounding; provenance metadata details are only broadly supported.
sentence_reviews.notes: The claim is defensible, but direct provenance/citation-tracking evidence would be stronger.

sentence_reviews.text: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include External retrieval & ranking, Databases & information retrieval.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Provenance, citations & evidence tracking is modeled as depending on External retrieval & ranking, Databases & information retrieval and feeding downstream nodes such as Neural search, RAG & question answering, Memory lifecycle, forgetting & consolidation, Content credentials, signing & provenance.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
